title: Kyle Eastmond

Fullback|Fullback]] | ru_club1 = [[Bath Rugby|Bath]] | ru_year1start = 2011 | ru_year1end = 16 | ru_appearances1 = 98 | ru_tries1 = 16 | ru_goals1 = 0 | ru_fieldgoals1 = 0 | ru_points1 = 80 | ru_club2 = [[Wasps RFC|Wasps]] | ru_year2start = 2016 | ru_year2end = 18 | ru_appearances2 = 23 | ru_tries2 = 4 | ru_goals2 = 0 | ru_fieldgoals2 = 0 | ru_points2 = 20 | ru_club3 = [[Leicester Tigers]] | ru_year3start = 2018 | ru_year3end = 20 | ru_appearances3 = 31 | ru_tries3 = 3 | ru_goals3 = 0 | ru_fieldgoals3 = 0 | ru_points3 = 15 | ru_teamA = [[England national rugby union team|England]] | ru_yearAstart = 2013 | ru_yearAend = 14 | ru_appearancesA = 6 | ru_triesA = 1 | ru_goalsA = 0 | ru_fieldgoalsA = 0 | ru_pointsA = 5 | coachteam1 = [[Halifax Panthers]] | coachyear1start = 2025 | coachyear1end = present | coachgames1 = 45 | coachwins1 = 22 | coachdraws1 = 1 | coachlosses1 = 22 | retired = no | source = {{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/kyle-eastmond/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}{{Rugby league records |id=622 |title=Kyle Eastmond |access-date=27 January 2026}} | updated = {{date|{{REVISIONTIMESTAMP}}|DMY}} | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = [[The Radclyffe School]] | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Craig Eastmond]] (cousin)
[[Miles Greenwood]] (cousin) }} }} '''Kyle Ovid Eastmond''' (born 17 July 1989){{cite web|url=http://en.espn.co.uk/england/rugby/player/156773.html|publisher=ESPN|access-date=1 August 2018|title=ESPN profile}} is a former dual-code rugby international, who played for St Helens, Bath Rugby and Leicester Tigers. He also played for England on multiple occasions in both codes. He is the current head coach of [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. Eastmond played [[rugby union]] for [[Bath Rugby|Bath]], [[Wasps RFC|Wasps]] and the [[Leicester Tigers]] in [[Premiership Rugby]] and [[England national rugby union team|England]] at international level. Prior to 2011 he played [[rugby league]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level. ==Early life== Kyle attended the Parish Church CEVA School, Oldham and [[the Radclyffe School]], Oldham.{{citation|url=http://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/11/roughyeds-news/30811/roughyeds-in-state-of-flux|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724003404/http://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/11/roughyeds-news/30811/roughyeds-in-state-of-flux|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 July 2011|publisher=oldham-chronicle.co.uk|date=6 October 2009|access-date=20 October 2009|title=Roughyeds in 'state of flux'}} Eastmond is the cousin of former professional [[rugby league]] footballer; [[Miles Greenwood]].{{cite web|url=http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-fax-is-miles-better-for-me-greenwood-1-1916299|title=Rugby league: Fax is Miles better for me: Greenwood|publisher=Halifax Courier|date=15 August 2008|access-date=27 September 2016}} Kyle is also related to [[Sutton United F.C.|Sutton United]]'s [[Craig Eastmond]], who is his cousin. He has supported Manchester United since childhood. ==Rugby League career== ===Early career=== During high school Eastmond began his rugby career playing rugby league for Oldham St Annes but in 2007, Eastmond was promoted to training with the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] first team squad. Eastmond had been compared to [[Australian national rugby league team|Australian]] international [[Scott Prince]], because of his darting runs to the line and a good kicking game. He has also got a keen eye for a gap in the defensive line. ===2008-09=== In 2008, he was given the number 24 jersey and made a spectacular early impression in the traditional [[Good Friday]] fixture with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], scoring a late try and a goal.{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/mar/26/rugbyleague.sthelens1|title=Rugby league: Modest Eastmond on fast track to become new Jason Robinson|author=Guardian Staff|date=26 March 2008|access-date=31 January 2020|via=www.theguardian.com}} He made 13 appearances in this season scoring 6 tries. He was then compared to [[Jason Robinson (rugby)|Jason Robinson]] for his general attributes, and with his size finds it easy to duck under any oncoming opposition which helped him make 778m. [[File:Kyle Eastmond.JPG|thumb|right|upright=1.2|Eastmond playing for Saints against the [[Wigan Warriors]]]] In 2009, Eastmond became an influential player for the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] side. He may have played one less game than in 2008, but he scored 8 tries, 30 goals (18 more than the previous year), seven try assists, he gained 899m and made more clean breaks. Eastmond capped off an impressive year in 2009 by scoring all of Saints' 10 points in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|last=AFP|title=Leeds makes it Super League hat-trick|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]] News|date=11 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121113060836/http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|archive-date=13 November 2012}}{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|work=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}} He was then selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[2009 Four Nations]] tournament and made his international début against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in a warm-up game. In December 2009, Eastmond signed a new two-year contract with St Helens that ended at the end of the 2011 Super League season. ===2010-11=== After [[Sean Long]] left [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] to join [[Hull F.C.]] at the end of the 2009 season, Kyle became the new number 7 for Saints. He started the year very well before injuries hit him for the 2nd half of the season. Despite this, he still managed to make 19 appearances, scoring 15 tries, 63 goal kicks (while missing 18) and making two drop goals. He made a total of 1615m. He repeatedly aggravated past injuries forcing him to miss the [[2010 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. [[Matty Smith (rugby league)|Matty Smith]], now of [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], returned from his loan spell at Salford for the play-offs and featured in the final. In early 2011 Eastmond was linked with a move to [[Rugby Union]]. He put these rumours on hold when he started the season with Saints. However, he performed poorly and frustrated fans. On 28 February 2011 both the Saints, and coach [[Royce Simmons]], confirmed he was to leave Saints to join [[Bath Rugby]]. After the announcement, Eastmond promised to be 100% committed to his current club for the rest of the season.{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/dna/606/A81958594 |publisher=news.bbc.co.uk |date=28 February 2011 |access-date=28 February 2011 |title=Confirmed: Eastmond to Bath Rugby Union |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924205903/http://www.bbc.co.uk/dna/606/A81958594 |archive-date=24 September 2015}} Commenting on the move, Bath Director of Rugby [[Ian McGeechan|Sir Ian McGeechan]] said he envisions Eastmond playing at [[Centre (rugby union)|centre]]. On 16 March 2011 it was announced on the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] website that he had been suspended by the club pending an investigation into serious misconduct. The club followed this up by saying that no further comments were to be made until the completion of the investigation. It was later confirmed by the club that he had made an inappropriate gesture to his home fans, and it was unlikely he would ever play in the ‘Red V’ again, especially after the emergence of scrum-half sensation [[Jonny Lomax]], and stand-off [[Lee Gaskell]]. He was dropped to the under-18s team but after injuries struck Saints, Eastmond was called on to fill in. He had to play under difficult circumstances, with Saints fans booing him with every touch of the ball. Despite this, he made a decent return. In July 2011 he successfully made the switch from the halfbacks to fullback, scoring two tries against [[Wakefield Trinity]] on 9 July. After that, Eastmond regained his confidence and was playing in a similar way to the 2008-09 seasons. He managed 15 appearances and scored five tries, but his workload was reduced to just four goal kicks (three missed kicks), due to [[Jamie Foster]] replacing him as the club's kicker. [[File:Kyle Eastmond and Matthew Tait.jpg|thumb|right|upright=1.2|Eastmond playing for Bath, being tackled by Matthew Tait of the Leicester Tigers]] ===Leeds Rhinos=== On 3 March 2021 it was reported that he had signed for Leeds in the [[Super League]],{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=3 March 2021|access-date=3 March 2021|title=Kyle Eastmond: Leeds Rhinos sign dual code international on return to rugby league|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56252295}} however, on 17 May he announced his immediate retirement from rugby having played only two games for his new club. ==Rugby Union career== ===Domestic=== ====Bath==== Eastmond joined Bath, when he moved in October 2011, after his contract finished at Saints. At Bath he played well and picked up Rugby Union quickly meaning that in 2013, Eastmond was selected to play for the England Saxons. During the 13/14 season, Eastmond formed a crucial partnership with George Ford, which led to Baths resurgence to the top of Aviva Premiership, he would then get selected for the main England side in the 2014 Autumn internationals but missed out on selection to England's World Cup squad to then Bath Club colleague Sam Burgess. ====Wasps==== After a poor season by the whole team after the World Cup he left Bath to join Wasps who were emerging as top team and had bought a new stadium in the Ricoh Arena, Coventry. His period with Wasps was blighted by injury, and resultantly playing second-choice often from the bench to other more reliable players. ====Leicester==== On 1 August 2018 Leicester Tigers announced the signing of Eastmond.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/45030592|title=Kyle Eastmond: Leicester sign utility back|date=1 August 2018|access-date=1 August 2018|publisher=BBC}} He left ahead of the 2020–21 season.{{Cite news|date=1 July 2020|title=Leicester Tigers: Manu Tuilagi & Kyle Eastmond leave Premiership club|language=en-gb|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/53256761|access-date=2 July 2020}} ===International=== ====England==== Eastmond was selected in the [[England national rugby union team|England]] squad to tour America in the summer of 2013. He played his first game in the uncapped match against Barbarians on 26 May 2013, scoring a try shortly after coming off the bench as a replacement.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/22673139|title=BBC Sport - England 40-12 Barbarians: Freddie Burns shines in win|publisher=Bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2013|access-date=2 February 2014}} He won his first England cap as a replacement in the first test against [[Argentina national rugby union team|Argentina]].{{cite web|author=ChronSport|url=http://www.bathchronicle.co.uk/Kyle-Eastmond-earns-England-cap-victory-Argentina/story-19240468-detail/story.html|title=Bath Rugby back Kyle Eastmond earned his first England cap in the 32-3 victory over Argentina|publisher=Bath Chronicle|date=10 June 2013|access-date=2 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140221220759/http://www.bathchronicle.co.uk/Kyle-Eastmond-earns-England-cap-victory-Argentina/story-19240468-detail/story.html|archive-date=21 February 2014|url-status=dead}} Eastmond made his first start for England on 15 June 2013, replacing Lions call-up [[Billy Twelvetrees]]. He scored a try in the 51-26 victory over Argentina.{{cite web|last=Standley|first=James|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/22915126|title=BBC Sport - Argentina 26-51 England|publisher=Bbc.co.uk|date=15 June 2013|access-date=2 February 2014}} ==Coaching career== ===Halifax Panthers=== On 25 October 2024 he was announced as the new head coach of [[Halifax Panthers]]{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 October 2024|title=Kyle Eastmond completes rugby league return after becoming Championship club's head coach|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/kyle-eastmond-appointed-halifax-panthers-coach|author=Drew Darbyshire}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cq52e0yzee7o|title=Dual-code star Eastmond named new Halifax coach|publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=25 October 2024}} ==References== {{Reflist}}
